summary: Listener is in the final 4-week stretch before Maryland's June 23, 2026 primary. Focus on candidate-by-candidate biography vs. record, MCPS budget and the Wootton timeline, and clearer differentiation between the five Council-At-Large Democrats. Recent push: stop pre-political-bio-thin episodes from being repeated and surface line-item budget votes.
themes: MCPS line-item budget votes — listener wants per-vote granularity, not yes/no totals | matters because the Wootton High decision turns on it
Pre-political biography for incumbents whose recent record is well covered | matters because endorsement-context comes from career arc
Side-by-side ballot matrix for the five Council-At-Large Democrats | matters because differentiation is the listener's #1 ask
Sanctuary / ICE Out Act framing on individual candidates | matters because immigration vote alignment is unclear across the slate
MCPS rating methodology change and how Rockville schools graded | matters because school choice flows through this metric
open_questions: What is Ben Kramer's full historical voting record back to 2007 on consumer protection, judiciary, immigration, and education funding?
Is Andrew Friedson actually the fiscal-restraint candidate when measured by individual line-item votes?
Why is MCPS proposing to close/relocate Wootton High School and what happens next on the timeline?
Among the five+ Council-At-Large Democrats, which one (if any) actually opposes the existing sanctuary framework?
What is the new MCPS rating methodology and how is each Rockville school graded under it?
underserved_topics: Pre-political biographies of incumbents (Friedson, Glass, Jawando)
Judicial branch races (Circuit Court Judge, Orphans' Court)
Register of Wills / Clerk of Circuit Court — almost no coverage
MoCo Board of Education candidates beyond at-large slate
Republican alternative views on countywide races (the listener votes D primary, but wants to know what's on the general ballot)
avoid_list: 'Thirty-X days out from the Maryland Democratic primary' cold-open device — used 2026-05-16/17/18/19 ep1
'Montgomery County native' framing hook — used heavily 2026-05-16 Friedson ep1
'My Seven Black Fathers' / Reid Temple A.M.E. as memoir-anchor for anyone other than Jawando — used 2026-05-19 ep1
'Anniston, Alabama / Donoho School / Vaughn Stewart Junior as mayor' formative-experience device for anyone other than Vaughn Stewart — used 2026-05-20 ep1
'Born in Trenton / adopted as an infant from a teenage mom in a convent' origin device for anyone other than Dawn Luedtke — used 2026-05-17 ep1
'First Muslim officer of the Montgomery County Police Department' identity-formation device for anyone other than Sharif Hidayat — used 2026-05-18 ep1
'Pre-political biography is thinner in the public record / no Wikipedia entry adds independent verification' as candidate-specific qualifier — used 2026-05-16 ep1
ICE Out Act / Trust Act / DHS officer screening bill as the immigration-record framing hook — used 2026-05-21 Glass ep3/ep4
'Run the buses, teach the kids, pick up the trash' tagline — used 2026-05-21 Glass ep1/ep2/ep4
